# Task Topics

## Interacting with the User Interface

#### **To interact with the site as a whole**

The web application’s user interface (UI) enables the user to bring up new pages or traverse the website to investigate the data. There are several tabs at the top of the website that allow the user to navigate through different pages and interact with various toggles, dropdown menus, and sliders. 

**Note** that the blue, clickable links on the UI, which enable the user to navigate between pages of the website, will be referred to as “tabs” for the remainder of this document. Each tab is circled in the screenshot of the **Home** page below. The tab label that is not blue indicates the user’s current page. 

![][image1]

1. Open a web browser of your choice (Firefox, Chrome, Edge, etc.).  
2. Paste “[https://cholstro.shinyapps.io/shiny-music/](https://cholstro.shinyapps.io/shiny-music/)” into the URL/search bar and hit *Enter*. You will see the image above once the website loads.  
3. Select the **Number One Albums** tab to navigate to the “Number One Albums” page.  
4. Select the **Top Albums by Year** tab to navigate to the “Top Albums by Year” page.  
5. Select the **Artists’ Albums** tab to navigate to the “Artists’ Albums” page.  
6. Select the **Favorite Artists** tab to navigate to the “Favorite Artists” page.  
7. Select the **Artist Comparison** tab to navigate to the “Artist Comparison” page.  
8. Select the **Vinyl** tab to navigate to the “Vinyl” page.  
9. Select the **Home** tab to navigate to the “Home” page.

#### **To interact with the “Number One Albums” page**

This page enables the user to filter the data by years using a dual-range slider. The constraints of the slider are as follows:

- The left circle (minimum year) can not be farther right (greater than) than the right circle (maximum year).  
- The right circle (maximum year) can not be farther left (less than) than the left circle (minimum year).

1. Select and hold the left or the right circle on the timeline.  
2. Drag the circle to a new location on the timeline within the slider’s constraints. You should see the table’s output change below the slider. For example, see the two images below:

![][image2]![][image3]

#### **To interact with the “Top Albums by Year” page**

This page enables the user to show the top albums for a specific year using a dropdown menu. 

**Note** that you will not need to click the **Submit** button again after the first time you click it if you want to re-filter the data. 

1. Click the dropdown menu box, outlined in red in the image below.![][image4]  
2. Click the year that you want to display.  
3. Click the **Submit** button. A table appears with data under the **Submit** button.  
4. Repeat steps 1 and 2 if you want to filter the data again.

#### **To interact with the “Artists’ Albums” page**

This page enables the user to filter the data by band or artist name using a dropdown menu. 

**Note** that you will not need to click the **Submit** button again after the first time you click it if you want to re-filter the data:

1. Click the dropdown menu box, outlined in red in the image below:![][image5]  
2. Click the band or artist name that you want to filter the data by. Alternatively, you can also delete the selected artist name and type out an artist’s name. Then hit **Enter** on your keyboard.  
3. Click the **Submit** button. A table appears with data under the **Submit** button.  
4. Repeat steps 1 and 2 if you want to filter the data again.

#### **To interact with the “Favorite Artists” page**

This page enables the user to filter the data in two ways: filter the data by the minimum number of albums per artist using a dropdown menu and a checkbox to exclude EPs and live albums. 

**Note** that you will not need to click the **Submit** button again after the first time you click it if you want to re-filter the data. 

1. Click the dropdown menu box, outlined in red in the image below:![][image6]  
2. Click the number of albums that you want to filter the data by.  
3. Optionally, click the square box to the left of the text **Exlude EPs and Live Albums** to enable or disable the filter.  
4. Click the **Submit** button. A table appears with data under the **Submit** button.  
5. Repeat steps 1, 2, and optionally 3 if you want to filter the data again.

#### **To interact with the “Artist Comparison” page**

This page enables the user to filter the data interactively for a visualization.

**Note** that some selections of bands or artists will not change the graph that much, depending on how many albums they have. For example, artists with a single album will only have a single dot appear on the graph, where artists with more albums will have a dot for each of their albums. 

1. Click on the first dropdown menu box, outlined in red in the image below:![][image7]  
2. Click the band or artist that you want to filter the data by. Alternatively, you can also delete the selected artist name and type out an artist’s name. Then hit **Enter** on your keyboard. The graph changes slightly to reflect the new filter.  
3. Click on the second dropdown menu box, outlined in blue in the image above.  
4. Click the band or artist that you want to filter the data by. Alternatively, you can also delete the selected artist name and type out an artist’s name. The graph changes slightly to reflect the new filter.  
5. Repeat steps 1 and 2, and/or steps 3 and 4 if you want to filter the data again.

#### **To interact with the “Vinyl” page**

This page enables the user to find albums that are not owned on vinyl based on their ratings using a dropdown menu. The user selects a rating range from the dropdown menu, and non-vinyl albums that have that rating or higher populate the table below the **Submit** button.

**Note** that you will not need to click the **Submit** button again after the first time you click it if you want to re-filter the data. 

1. Click the dropdown menu box, outlined in red in the image below:![][image8]  
2. Click the album’s rating that you want to filter the data by.  
3. Click the **Submit** button. A table appears with data under the **Submit** button.  
4. Repeat steps 1 and 2 if you want to filter the data again.

## Refreshing or Reloading the Web Application

If the web application is not interacted with for \~15 minutes, the Shiny App server will shut down. This results in the UI turning dark grey and not being interactive. There are two distinct solutions to this problem, should it arise.

#### **To refresh or reload the web application**

1. Reload the web page from the browser using either of these options.  
   1. Refresh the browser tab with *cmd \+ r* (macOS/Linux) or *ctrl \+ r* (Windows)   
   2. Manually click the browser’s refresh button. The button will typically appear as an array in a circle near the top of the browser window, next to the URL/search bar.   
2. Click the purple **Reload** link from the dialog pop-up that appears in the bottom left corner of the screen, shown below:  
   ![][image9]
